title: Q1 2026 shatters records: $300B in global venture funding, 80% to AI
summary: Crunchbase reports the biggest venture quarter ever — $300B across 6,000 startups, up 150% YoY. AI alone captured $242B. Four of the five largest rounds in history closed in Q1, led by OpenAI's $122B and Anthropic's $30B.

title: Google Gemini 3.1 Ultra: 2M-token native multimodal
summary: Google's biggest model release of 2026 handles text, image, audio and video natively across a 2M-token window — with a sandboxed code execution tool that lets the model write, run and test code mid-conversation.

title: AI just sparked a quantum breakthrough — 'the world is not ready'
summary: Time reports an AI-driven quantum advance that materially shortens the timeline to useful quantum computing. The discovery has immediate implications for cryptography, materials science and drug discovery.

title: Meta debuts Muse Spark — first major model since the $14B Scale deal
summary: Alexandr Wang's Meta Superintelligence Labs ships its first flagship. The release is the clearest signal yet of where Meta's record-breaking AI spend is pointed: agentic, multimodal, and built to catch OpenAI and Google.

title: Four of the five largest VC rounds in history closed this quarter
summary: OpenAI ($122B), Anthropic ($30B), xAI ($20B) and Waymo ($16B) collectively raised $188B — 65% of all global venture investment in Q1. D.E. Shaw and MGX co-led OpenAI and Anthropic, a combined $150B+ commitment.

title: Google patches 4th actively-exploited Chrome zero-day of 2026
summary: An out-of-band Chrome 146 release fixes CVE-2026-5281 — already being exploited in the wild. Update immediately: it's the fourth Chrome zero-day patched in just over three months.

title: Quantum error detection in milliseconds — a leap toward reliable QC
summary: A new detection technique identifies qubit errors orders of magnitude faster than prior methods. Combined with a separate breakthrough measuring data loss 100× faster, researchers can now track errors near real-time.

title: Anthropic's MCP hits 97M installs; Linux Foundation takes governance
summary: Model Context Protocol has become the de facto standard for connecting AI to tools and data. Every major AI provider now ships MCP-compatible tooling. The Linux Foundation taking open governance cements it as critical open infrastructure.

title: Fault-tolerant Grover's algorithm — no mid-circuit measurements needed
summary: Innsbruck and Aachen teams demonstrated a universal fault-tolerant quantum algorithm without mid-circuit measurements, running Grover's search on 3 logical qubits on a trapped-ion processor. A major simplification of fault-tolerant QC.

title: Vulnerability exploits overtake phishing as #1 initial access method
summary: Cisco Talos: nearly 40% of Q4 2025 intrusions came from exploited flaws — surpassing phishing for the first time. The shift reflects how AI-assisted exploit development has collapsed the time-to-weaponize for new CVEs.

title: Citrix NetScaler CVE-2026-3055 under active exploit
summary: Attackers are actively reading sensitive memory from NetScaler ADC and Gateway appliances — enough to steal session material and chain into rapid follow-on access. Patch immediately; rotate sessions.

title: Eclipse raises $1.3B for robotics and AI infrastructure
summary: The firm best known for backing Cerebras closed $1.3B to double down on physical AI — robotics, data center build-outs, and the compute substrate underneath the agentic AI wave.

title: MIT AI model uncovers atomic defects for better chips and solar
summary: The model identifies atomic-scale defects in materials that affect heat transfer and energy conversion — immediate implications for semiconductor yield and renewable-energy efficiency.

title: Google's TurboQuant slashes KV cache memory at ICLR 2026
summary: A two-step quantization algorithm — PolarQuant random rotation plus Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss compression — materially reduces the memory overhead of long-context inference. Implications for on-device AI and data center cost.

title: OpenAI hits $852B valuation; Anthropic at $380B
summary: OpenAI closed its $122B round at $852B post-money on March 31, making it the most valuable standalone startup ever. Anthropic's Series G put it at $380B — both backed heavily by D.E. Shaw and MGX.

title: Reddit shuts down r/all; moves to algorithmic feed
summary: The unfiltered discovery feed that built Reddit culture is gone — officially deprecated April 2 in favor of algorithmic personalization. The era of 'what the internet is talking about, raw' just ended on one of the last open platforms.

title: Neutral-atom quantum computing: 2026's big leap
summary: IEEE Spectrum on why neutral-atom approaches — led by Atom Computing and partners — are emerging as the architecture to watch. Microsoft plans to deliver an error-corrected level-2 neutral-atom system to customers this year.

title: Physics-informed ML: AI that respects the laws of physics
summary: University of Hawaiʻi researchers published an algorithm that forces AI to adhere to physical laws while processing complex datasets — opening the door to credible AI for fluid dynamics and climate modeling.

title: Google & Oratomic: encryption-breaking quantum may arrive sooner
summary: New papers shorten timelines for cryptographically relevant quantum computers. Cloudflare responded by accelerating its post-quantum roadmap — full system-wide resilience now targeted for 2029.

title: Karpathy on LLM knowledge bases with Obsidian — 18K likes
summary: Andrej Karpathy's practical take on combining personal Obsidian vaults with LLMs struck a nerve — turning a niche workflow into a widely-shared playbook for personal AI.
